t_ch: 座钟停留在十点十分，你好奇这背后的深意。
t_en: The clock is stuck at <unbreak>10</unbreak>:<unbreak>10</unbreak>. You wonder if there's any hidden meaning behind this.

t_ch: 也许十点十分是贝洛伯格历史上最重要的时间？也许就在那一刻，反物质军团突袭了这颗星球；也许星核在那一个瞬间爆发；也许……
t_en: Maybe <unbreak>10</unbreak>:<unbreak>10</unbreak> is the most critical moment in the history of Belobog? Perhaps it was during this moment when the Antimatter Legion raided the planet. Maybe the Stellaron exploded at this moment. Or could it be...

t_ch: 也许换个角度思考，十点十分更多是象征意义。十，是一个大数，是十进制转换位的末点。十点十分意味着旧时代的终结，新时代的开始。
t_en: Let's look at things from another perspective, <unbreak>10</unbreak>:<unbreak>10</unbreak> might be more symbolic in meaning. The number ten, well it's a large one for sure, and it's the endpoint for decimal numbers. So <unbreak>10</unbreak>:<unbreak>10</unbreak> could also mean the end of an Old Era and the beginning of a new one.

t_ch: 停！别再做阅读理解了！有没有一种可能，我是说可能，这些钟压根就不会走，钟面只是个装饰呢？
t_en: Hold your horses! This isn't Shakespeare! Is there a possibility, and by that, I mean the remotest possibility, that these clocks aren't actually running and are just decorations?

t_ch: 你不知道真正的答案。座钟依然停留在十点十分。
t_en: Ultimately, you will never know the answer. The clock is still stuck at <unbreak>10</unbreak>:<unbreak>10</unbreak>.

t_ch: 筒口露出两只信封，它们保持着微妙的平衡：触碰任何一只，都会让另一只被漆黑的大口吞没。如此两难的抉择竟落在你的面前。
t_en: The opening of the post box reveals two envelopes that are being kept in a delicate balance. Touching either one will condemn the other to be swallowed by the pitch-black maw. Such a dilemma falls in front of you.

t_ch: 拿走左边的信封。
t_en: Take the envelope on the left.

t_ch: 拿走右边的信封。
t_en: Take the envelope on the right.

t_ch: 全推进去。
t_en: Push them all in.

t_ch: 尝试全拿出来。<color=#dbc291ff>「智识等级≥1」</color>
t_en: Try to take them all out.<color=#dbc291ff> "Erudition level ≥ 1"</color>

t_ch: 不管它们。
t_en: Leave them be.

t_ch: 你收起信封。
t_en: You take the envelope.

t_ch: 你伸出手，轻轻碰触——甚至不知道自己碰到了哪里，两只信封的受力平衡瞬间被打破。做好事的感觉竟然如此美妙。
t_en: You reach your hand out and gently touch them. You aren't sure where your hand skimmed, but the balance of the two envelopes is instantly broken. It's incredible how good it feels to do something good.

t_ch: 在你的眼中，邮筒和两只信封构成了美丽的数学图景。力——银河中不可见不可触，却无处不在的神秘能量化作实体，在你的脑中构筑模型。
t_en: To you, the mailbox and the two envelopes form a beautiful mathematical picture. The concept of force — the invisible, untouchable, but ubiquitous phenomenon that permeates throughout the cosmos. A model coalesces inside your mind.

t_ch: 通过精密的计算，你找到了唯一的解法：左信封斜角<unbreak>45</unbreak>°向下施加<unbreak>30</unbreak>利尔他，从而使右信封的重心落在邮筒外。就这样，你得到了两只信封。
t_en: Through precise calculations, you arrive at the only solution: Applying <unbreak>30</unbreak> Aiden at a <unbreak>45</unbreak>° angle to the left envelope will make the center of gravity of the right envelope fall outside the mailbox. This way, you get both envelopes.

t_ch: 你耸耸肩，离它而去。世界上每时每刻都有人出生，患病，死去，宇宙中未被妥投的邮件数以亿计，这两只又特殊在哪里？总有人会来处理它——其实，你很确定，当你下次来到这个邮筒时，就不会再看见它们了。
t_en: You shrug your shoulders and walk away from it. Every minute, new people are born into this world, suffering from sickness or passing away. There are billions of undelivered pieces of mail in the universe, so what makes these two so special? Someone else will come along and take care of them — in fact, you're pretty sure you won't see them the next time you come by this mailbox.

t_ch: 邮筒对你张开黑漆漆的筒口，就像嗷嗷待哺的婴孩。
t_en: The mailbox opens its black mouth to you, like a baby waiting to be fed.

t_ch: 你打开信封，信上写着：亲爱的卡尔•艾尔先生，您这个月的工作资料在我手里。别担心，它现在很安全。
t_en: You open the letter, which reads: Dear Mr. Carl Eyre, your work documents for this month are with me. Don't worry, they are quite safe.

t_ch: 如果您想要它平安回来，请在本周六下午三点，把<unbreak>100</unbreak>元藏在你家壁橱顶上被夫人锁住的箱子里那根钓竿的杆身内，带着这根钓竿出门，我会再和你联系。
t_en: If you want them returned to you in the same condition, hide <unbreak>100</unbreak> shield within the shaft of the fishing rod inside the chest locked by your wife on top of your wardrobe. At 3:<unbreak>00</unbreak> PM this Saturday, leave your home with the fishing rod and I will contact you again.

t_ch: 听好了，这件事你可以告诉你的夫人，但不许报警，不然你这个月的工作成果将会变成一桶废纸，领导会怎么看待你？相信夫人一定不愿发生这种事吧。
t_en: Feel free to tell your wife about this, but refrain from raising the alarm... unless you want your achievements for the month to become confetti? I'm not sure your boss or your wife would like that to happen.

t_ch: 这只邮筒仿佛饱经沧桑，你隐约看见两片夹着红肠的面包被人当作信件塞进邮筒——要么，就是这只邮筒被当作垃圾箱塞入了垃圾。
t_en: This mailbox looks like it's seen better days. You think you saw someone post a sausage between two bits of bread here... Come to think of it, maybe they were using it as a trashcan.

t_ch: 这是一座电话亭，人们用它和远方的朋友联系。
t_en: This is a phone booth. People use it to contact friends from afar.

t_ch: 这是一座电话亭，你觉得它很新奇，因为你出生在一个远程通讯设备可以手持的时代。
t_en: This is a phone booth. You find it novel because you were born in the mobile era.

t_ch: 你站在电话亭旁，想起自己曾经看过的幻想剧——你不禁好奇在电话亭里撕开衬衫是什么感觉。
t_en: You stand next to the booth and start thinking about the fantasy shows you used to watch. You can't help wondering how ripping your shirt off in a phone booth would feel.

t_ch: 看见电话亭，你觉得自己好像能变身为超级英雄。
t_en: Looking at this phone booth, you feel like you could transform into a superhero.

t_ch: 你只来过行政区，因此你很好奇：贝洛伯格究竟有多大？有多少需要依靠电话才能联络的人们？
t_en: You've only visited the Administrative District so far, so you start to wonder how big Belobog really is? How many people rely on phones to contact each other?

t_ch: 你靠近这只小小的设备，感到些微暖意。
t_en: You approach this small device, and feel a slight warmth emanating.

t_ch: 加热器散发着温暖，它虽不起眼，却是冬城人民得以生存的功臣。
t_en: The heater emits warmth. It's inconspicuous, but the people of the Union can't live without it.

t_ch: 从地底采掘出的地髓矿石在这里转化成了人们赖以为生的热量。
t_en: Here, Geomarrow ore excavated from underground is converted into heat necessary for survival.

t_ch: 你接近时，感到温暖仿佛乖巧的宠物轻轻磨蹭你的腿部。
t_en: As you approach, you feel the warmth gently around your legs, as if an affectionate pet were rubbing up against them.

t_ch: 当初设计者一定花费了巨大的心力，才让供暖设备遍布在这座永屹之城。
t_en: Extending this heating network across the Towering Citadel must have been a huge effort for the designer.

t_ch: 小小的加热器伫立着，以微弱的力量抵抗大自然的寒冷气流。
t_en: The little heater stands alone, resisting nature's biting winds with its faint power.

t_ch: 这是街边的路灯。
t_en: This is a street lamp.

t_ch: 路灯还没有打开。
t_en: The street lamps haven't been turned on yet.

t_ch: 你想起某个古老世界的刑罚：在那里，失德之人身披黑色的斗篷，被挂在路灯上。
t_en: You remember a punishment from an ancient world, where those judged to have lost their virtue would be put up on street lamps draped in black cloaks.

t_ch: 在其它的世界里，路灯是鸟儿喜爱的栖息地；而在贝洛伯格，路灯是如此孤零零。
t_en: In other worlds, street lamps make wonderful roosts for birds, but in Belobog, they stand all alone.

t_ch: 当夜晚来临时，一盏盏点亮的路灯将为人们指明回家的路。
t_en: When night draws near, street lamps light up the way home.

t_ch: 你好奇路灯是否也使用地髓进行供暖。
t_en: You're curious to know whether street lamps also rely on Geomarrow and give off warmth.

t_ch: 这根路灯的金属杆上已经有了裂痕。
t_en: The metal pole of this lamp has already begun to crack.

t_ch: 路灯上斑斑的污迹诉说着它的历史。
t_en: The marks on this lamp are clues to its history.

t_ch: 书随便挑，《水晶日报》不带一份走么？
t_en: Pick any book. Why not take a copy of the Crystal Daily?

t_ch: 新书到货，仅剩一册！
t_en: New arrivals, only one copy left!

t_ch: 大红色的邮筒，在冰冷的贝洛伯格艳如烈火。
t_en: The big red mailboxes are like burning torches in the frosts of Belobog.

t_ch: 小小的邮筒，将贝洛伯格各区彼此相连。
t_en: The small mailboxes connect the districts of Belobog.

t_ch: 寄往边境高墙的信件投递最晚——大约需要两天。
t_en: Mail sent to the border wall will be delivered last. It will take around two days.

t_ch: 长椅上结了一层冰，怪不得没有人坐。
t_en: There is a layer of ice on the bench. No wonder nobody wants to sit there.

t_ch: 人来人往的大街上，空荡荡的长椅显得极为落寞。
t_en: The empty benches look lonely on the busy street.

t_ch: 长椅的设计非常符合人体工学，但它们的投放者显然没有考虑环境的因素。
t_en: The benches are ergonomic in design, but their placement doesn't take the environment into consideration.

t_ch: 「你的心要像贝洛伯格的长椅一样冰冷。」——诗人{NICKNAME}，作于贝洛伯格冬日。
t_en: "Your heart must be as cold as the benches in Belobog." — Poet {NICKNAME}, written in Belobog during winter.

t_ch: 关于「你」的流言在城市内纷纷扬扬：据说一个陌生人翻遍了全城的垃圾桶，像在寻找，又像挽留。
t_en: Rumors are spreading around the city about "you." The whispers speak of a stranger that has rummaged through all the trashcans in the city — as if in search of something, trying to prevent something from being lost.

t_ch: 陌生人孤身来到这座城市，他的面容坚毅，他的动作迟缓，他翻找垃圾桶的模样，就像拥抱失落的爱人。
t_en: The stranger came to the city by himself. His face is stoic, and his movements sluggish. He rummaged through the trashcans as if he was embracing a lost lover.

t_ch: 在飞舞的传言中，「你」的形象逐渐清晰…
t_en: Amidst the dancing whispers, the image of "you" gradually became more clear...

t_ch: 「你」是一个瘦削的短发男人，在对抗裂界怪物的战斗中失去了听力；
t_en: "You" are a slim and short-haired man who lost his hearing during a battle against a Fragmentum monster.

t_ch: 「你」的一只眼睛是假的，装着冷凝地髓液做成的假眼球；
t_en: One of "your" eyes is a fake eyeball made of condensed Geomarrow fluid.

t_ch: 你握着一柄长枪，它既是拐杖，也是你的兄弟……
t_en: You hold a spear, which is both your crutch and your dear comrade...

t_ch: 你忍不住看了丹恒一眼。似乎他默默为你扛下了所有。
t_en: You can't help looking at Dan Heng. He seems to be silently carrying everything on his shoulders for you.

t_ch: 这是幻想剧《路边野餐》的宣传画。
t_en: This is a poster for the fantasy play Roadside Picnic.

t_ch: 你看到《路边野餐》中的主角是一群地鼠，不禁对剧作家的品味产生了一丝丝的质疑。但是随着剧情简介的深入，你很快意识到这才是整部剧的天才巧思。
t_en: After finding out the main characters in Roadside Picnic are a bunch of gophers, you can't help but doubt the playwright's taste ever so slightly. But after reading further into the story's synopsis, you soon realize that this is the stroke of genius behind the play.

t_ch: 安东一家去近郊的路边随意野餐，却给地鼠的王国带来了诡谲怪异的宝物。而当安东一家离开时，争斗着宝物归属的地鼠王国又恢复了往日和平。
t_en: The Anton family went to the suburbs for a casual picnic but, in doing so, brought a treacherous and strange treasure to the kingdom of gophers. However, after the Anton family left, the gopher kingdom that once fought over the ownership of the treasure returned to its previous peace.

t_ch: 你不禁将自己看作是地鼠王国的一员，人类无意在你的领地上留下了各式的野餐垃圾。你们为这些难以理解的科技献出了生命，但人类似乎从未注意到你们。
t_en: You can't help but see yourself as a member of the gopher kingdom. The humans are haphazardly leaving all kinds of picnic trash in your territory. You give your lives to these incomprehensible technical products, but humans never seem to notice you.

t_ch: 这只是一次路边野餐，在贝洛伯格郊外的某条小路上，地鼠们却在好奇着安东会不会回去。
t_en: For the humans, it was nothing more than a roadside picnic somewhere in the Belobog's suburbia. However, the gophers are stuck wondering if Anton will ever return.

t_ch: 这是童话剧《蕾莎与特鲁太太》的宣传画。
t_en: A poster for the fairy-tale play Leisha and Mrs. Trude.

t_ch: 你注意到海报上刻意隐去了特鲁太太那可怖的造型：她骨瘦嶙峋，头发凌乱，留着长长的指甲，穿着破布一样的衣服…没能在宣传画上登场，也许是出于票房的考虑。
t_en: You notice Mrs. Trude is missing from the poster. Perhaps her skeletal appearance, frightful hair, long nails, and tattered clothes aren't the best recipe for a box office hit.

t_ch: 在去往老巫婆的家的路上，蕾莎遇见的三位骑士被老巫婆唤作：「我的黎明」、「我的太阳」与「我的黑夜」，特鲁太太仿佛对广阔的宇宙都拥有支配权。
t_en: On the way to the old witch's house, Leisha meets three knights. The witch calls them "my dawn," "my sun," and "my night.." It's as if Mrs. Trude is able to control everything in the vast universe.

t_ch: 你注意到好奇心旺盛的小孩在童话中总是有一些不幸的结局，而蕾莎不太一样，她似乎懂得把握好奇心的程度，在故事最后幸免于难。
t_en: You notice curious children tend to end up encountering misfortune in these stories, but Leisha is different. She knows how to control her curiosity and therefore lives to see another day at the end of the play.

t_ch: 尽管你从未看过这个童话剧，你却觉得它似曾相识。也许在不同世界不同剧作家的笔下，童话其实讲的是同一个故事。
t_en: Even though you have never seen this play before, you feel a sense of déjà vu. Perhaps the same fairy tale is being written by different playwrights from different worlds.

t_ch: 这是历史正剧《雪国往事》的宣传画。
t_en: This is the advertising poster for the historical drama Once Upon a Snowsieged Country.

t_ch: 筑城者们建立贝洛伯格的故事总是被人们津津乐道，扮演阿丽萨•兰德的演员也换了一代又一代，形成了光荣的传承。
t_en: The story of the Architects who built Belobog is always a popular one. The actors who played Alisa Rand have changed from version to version, creating a glorious legacy.

t_ch: 你发现《雪国往事》的宣传画展示了惊人的舞台美术装置，那是「存护」祝福的贝洛伯格城墙，在飞雪造景中屹立不倒。
t_en: You notice the advertisement poster for Once Upon a Snowsieged Country showcases a fantastic set-piece, which is none other than Belobog's walls blessed by Preservation standing tall in the snowy scenery.

t_ch: 你听说了《雪国往事》是历史悠久的传统歌剧，正是筑城者的精神塑造了一代又一代的贝洛伯格人。
t_en: You've heard that Once Upon a Snowsieged Country is a time-honored traditional opera. And it is none other than the spirit of the city's Architects that have shaped Belobog's people for generations to come.

t_ch: 这是爱情剧《星尘玫瑰》的宣传画。
t_en: This is an advertisement poster for the romantic drama Stardust Rose.

t_ch: 你发现人们总是在爱情剧的宣传画下成双入对，贝洛伯格这么大，你却只感觉到拥挤。
t_en: You notice that people always arrive in pairs under the advertisement poster of romantic dramas. Despite Belobog's vastness, you can only feel a sense of claustrophobia.

t_ch: 失意的公司职员在边荒小镇遇见真爱，面临事业与爱情的抉择。看完《星尘玫瑰》的剧情简介，你好像已经看完了整部剧。
t_en: A disillusioned company employee meets his true love in a small town on the edge of the desert and faces the choice between career and love. After reading the plot synopsis of Stardust Rose, you seem to have finished the whole drama.

t_ch: 「三年前陪你看《贝城假日》的人，今年还会陪你看《星尘玫瑰》吗？」你看着宣传语陷入了沉思。
t_en: "Will the person who watched Holiday in Belobog with you three years ago still be here to watch Stardust Rose with you this year?" You look at the tagline and get lost in thought.

t_ch: 虽然《星尘玫瑰》在逻辑上漏洞百出，但你也知道没人是奔着剧情来的。梦幻的时光稍纵即逝，在剧院里悄然度过显然是最好的选择。
t_en: Although Stardust Rose is full of plotholes, you know that no one is watching it for the plot. The dreamlike times are transient and fleeting, and spending it quietly in the theatre is clearly the best choice.

t_ch: 这是悬疑剧《白衣之后》的宣传画。
t_en: This is an advertisement poster for the suspense drama Beneath the White Cloak.

t_ch: 在化装舞会上宾客们接连遇害原本只是常见的命题创作，但是宣传画上不起眼的一行「根据真实事件改编」勾起了你的兴趣……
t_en: Guests being killed one after another in a masquerade party is quite a typical setup. However, an inconspicuous line on the poster saying "based on real events" aroused your interest...

t_ch: 你常常幻想自己也处在舞会之中，要是遇到这样不请自来的客人，以你的不俗身手，肯定能摘下她的冰雪面具，让故事在第一幕就早早结束……
t_en: You often envision yourself in the middle of such a scenario. If you encounter uninvited guests like that, you are confident that you can remove her ice mask with your exceptional dexterity to end the story in the first act...

t_ch: 你对悬疑剧的关注引起一旁小孩儿的注意，他带着一脸「我要剧透你」的得意坏笑朝着你走了过来，快跑！
t_en: Your focus on the suspense drama seems to attract the attention of a kid that's sitting nearby. He's running to you with a devious smile and an expression that clearly says, "I'm gonna drop some spoilers on that person." Let's high tail it out of here!

t_ch: 你看见《水晶日报》，它是筑城者的官方刊物。
t_en: You spot the Crystal Daily, which is the official publication for the Architects.

t_ch: 你看见《水晶日报》，架子上的它缺了几期。
t_en: You spot the Crystal Daily. It is missing a few issues on the shelf.

t_ch: 你看见《水晶日报•周末版》，上面有笑话版，显然周末的报纸不那么严肃。
t_en: You spot the Crystal Daily — Weekend Edition. There's also a section for jokes. Apparently, the weekend issue isn't so serious.

t_ch: 你看见《水晶日报•周末版》，它已经不剩几张了。
t_en: You spot the Crystal Daily — Weekend Edition. There are not many copies left behind.

t_ch: 你看见《远行者》，上面刊载的旅游指南犹如奇幻小说。
t_en: You spot the Voyager, which serializes travel guides written in the manner of fantasy novels.

t_ch: 你看见《远行者》，上面刊载了一篇堪比幻想生物图鉴的野兽观察。
t_en: You spot the Voyager, which contains an article about wildlife observation that is written in the manner of a bestiary for mythical creatures.

t_ch: 你看见《贝洛伯格周刊》，封面是一张颇为诱惑的画片。
t_en: You spot a Belobog Weekly with a rather seductive picture on the cover.

t_ch: 你看见《贝洛伯格周刊》，刊登的美食图片令你食欲大增。
t_en: You spot a Belobog Weekly. The pictures of food whet your appetite.

t_ch: 坏掉的加热器（家用版）。
t_en: Broken heater (home version).

t_ch: 你看见一种细细长长的植物，杆茎呈柱形，内有孔洞。
t_en: You see a tall but thin plant with columnar-shaped stems, the inside full of holes.

t_ch: 行李随意地堆在一旁。
t_en: The luggage was piled up randomly on the side.

t_ch: 珍贵的行李就这样无人看管地放着——这座城市拥有路不拾遗的优良品德。
t_en: Unattended precious luggage — the city has the exemplary virtue of not picking up things off the road.

t_ch: 据说在某些世界，某些星球，你可以随意拿走别人的财产而不受任何惩罚——但在这里不行。
t_en: Apparently, in some of the worlds and some planets, you can freely take other people's property with no penalty — but certainly not here.

t_ch: 作为星穹列车的一员，你很优秀，你知道别人的行李属于他人财产，神圣而不可侵犯。
t_en: As a member of Astral Express, you are well-mannered and respectful. You know that other people's luggage is their sacred and inviolable property.

t_ch: 你静静地倾听，行李没有发出微弱的嘀嗒声。你松了口气。
t_en: You inch in closer and quietly listen. The luggage doesn't make a faint ticking sound. You breathe a sigh of relief.

t_ch: 行李在等待着它们的主人。也许很快会有一位侦探打开它，拿走重要的线索，拯救整座城市——那是另一个故事了。
t_en: The luggage is waiting for its owner. Maybe shortly after, a detective will open it to discover essential clues and save the entire city — well, that's a whole new story.

t_ch: 这儿有桌子，椅子，和一张招牌。根据招牌上的画和文字：你可以在这里喝咖啡。
t_en: There are tables, chairs, and a signboard. According to the signage and text, you can drink coffee here.

t_ch: 你可以把随身行李寄存在这里——无人看守，全凭自觉。
t_en: You can store your carry-on luggage here. The luggage is unguarded, so everything is up to the self-discipline of the people.

t_ch: 巨大的星球仪。
t_en: A giant armillary sphere.

t_ch: 看到这个星球仪，你才想起来这里是一颗星球。
t_en: After seeing this armillary sphere, you realize that you are actually on a planet right now.

t_ch: 考虑到雅利洛-Ⅵ的居民数百年来都被困在一座城里，你可以理解这个星球仪为何如此受重视。
t_en: Considering that the inhabitants of Jarilo VI have been trapped in this city for centuries, you can understand why this armillary sphere is so highly regarded.

t_ch: 巨大的办公桌，非常干净。
t_en: A large office desk that's kept very clean.

t_ch: 桌面上的东西很少，通常情况下，这意味着桌子的主人即将离职。
t_en: There is very little on the desk, which usually means that the desk's owner is about to leave the job.

t_ch: 几本书、一盆花和些许文件，展示了办公桌主人的兴趣。
t_en: A few books, a potted flower, and some documents showcase the interests of the desk's owner.

t_ch: 非常精致的茶具，好像很久没人用过了。
t_en: A very fine tea set. It seems like no one has used it for a long time.

t_ch: 非常精致的罐子，不知道有什么用途。
t_en: A very delicate jar with an unknown purpose.

t_ch: 堆成了小山的文件，一定能卖不少钱吧。
t_en: A mound of piled-up documents. They must fetch for quite a bit of money.

t_ch: 文件堆成了小山，真的很不环保。
t_en: A mound of piled-up documents. What happened to being environmentally conscious?

t_ch: 堆成了小山的文件，显示出无纸化办公的必要性。
t_en: A mound of piled-up documents. They clearly demonstrate the need for a paperless office.

t_ch: 高大的书架，要是没书就更好了。
t_en: A tall bookshelf. It would be better if there were no books in it.

t_ch: 放着许多工具/机械的柜子。
t_en: A cabinet with many tools/machines.

t_ch: 柜子里放着千奇百怪的东西。
t_en: There are a multitude of strange objects in this cabinet.

t_ch: 放着工具的柜子。据说会制造和使用工具是人和动物最主要的区别。
t_en: A cabinet containing tools. It is said that the ability to make and use tools is the main difference between humans and animals.

t_ch: 柜子里放的东西，你只看外形都想象不出用途。
t_en: You cannot tell the function of the items inside the cabinet based on their silhouette.

t_ch: 老旧的录影机，它能放出什么你都不敢想象。
t_en: An old VCR machine. You do not dare imagine what it might show you.

t_ch: 齿轮们以违背构造学的方式转动着。
t_en: The gears are turning in a way that's violating the rules of tectonophysics.

t_ch: 湿垃圾！
t_en: Wet garbage!

t_ch: 错！千万不能把动物的毛发放进湿垃圾箱！
t_en: Nope! Never put animal fur in a wet trashcan!

t_ch: 不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 垃圾分类的知识涌入你的脑海。
t_en: Garbage-sorting knowledge comes flooding into your brain.

t_ch: 你沉浸在分类垃圾的快乐里……
t_en: You immerse yourself in the joys of sorting garbage...

t_ch: 几个黑色垃圾袋上洒落了碎纸屑。它碎得如此整齐，你还以为这个世界已经解锁了碎纸机的科技。
t_en: Scraps of paper are scattered all over the black garbage bags. They've been torn up so evenly that you begin to wonder whether this world may have discovered paper-shredding technology.

t_ch: 拼好。
t_en: Piece them back together.

t_ch: 喂…你要把这个都拼起来？
t_en: Huh...? You're gonna piece them all back together?

t_ch: 不知过了多久，你总算完成了这项伟业：无数纸片摇身变成了一张皱皱巴巴的纸，上面写着某位市民寄给另一位市民的有违伦常不被世俗所接受的信件。
t_en: After who knows how long, you finally complete your great undertaking: Countless paper scraps have become a wrinkled piece of paper. It was a letter from one citizen to another, but the radical content runs counter to standard notions of human hierarchy.

t_ch: 信的内容实在太可怕了，以至于你刚读完，它就碎成飞灰，再也无法还原。
t_en: The content is so scary that the letter disintegrates and drifts away on the wind as you finish reading it — never to be restored again.

t_ch: 置之不理。
t_en: Ignore.

t_ch: 是的，没必要浪费这个时间。
t_en: Right, no point wasting time.

t_ch: 一股腥味在你掀开垃圾桶盖时扑面而来，然而里面并没有鱼——只有一张和鱼有关的广告，莫非这就是所谓的通感？
t_en: An overwhelmingly fishy smell emanates from the trashcan as you remove the lid. Instead of fish, you find only a fish-related flyer. Is this how synesthesia works?

t_ch: 两个垃圾桶，一个锃光瓦亮地站着，一个锈迹斑斑地躺着。
t_en: Two trashcans. One stands glamorously shining, the other lies unceremoniously rusting.

t_ch: 你本想走近把它扶起，却终究没有这么做。因为垃圾桶也有身为垃圾桶的尊严。
t_en: You want to help it up, but think better of it. Even trashcans have their dignity.

t_ch: 垃圾桶里躺着一张漂亮的明信片，上面写着：「住手吧，只有以下几人有权翻动贝洛伯格的垃圾桶：塔塔洛夫，垃圾之王和我。」
t_en: A pretty postcard lies among the garbage. It reads: "Stay your hand. Only the following persons are permitted to go through Belobog's trashcans: Tatalov, Garbage King, and myself."

t_ch: 后面又写道：「和清洁队的<unbreak>45</unbreak>位工人。」明信片背面什么都没有。
t_en: It continues: "And the <unbreak>45</unbreak> members of the cleaning squad." There's nothing written on the back.

t_ch: 你一头雾水。
t_en: You are confused.

t_ch: 这城里的垃圾桶还真严肃。
t_en: Even trashcans are serious business in this city.

t_ch: 你在垃圾桶里找到一张皱巴巴的手写传单，传单最上方用娟秀的字体写着：「正当权益会社。」
t_en: You find a wrinkled handwritten flyer in the trashcan. Written at the top in beautiful handwriting you see: "The Rightful Rights Society."

t_ch: 内容大意是鼓动那些因家庭和责任感而放弃了自己的兴趣爱好的人加入这个奇怪的组织。
t_en: The content seems to be trying to convince people who have given up on interests and hobbies due to family and other responsibilities to join this strange organization.

t_ch: 组织内部会互相掩护，帮助成员在周末（甚至工作日）逃离家庭或公司，享受完全由自己主宰的一天。该组织称之为「伸张正当权益」。
t_en: Members of the organization cover for one another and help each other get away from family or work on weekends (and even weekdays) in the pursuit of individual freedom. They call it "upholding our rightful rights."

t_ch: 这什么奇怪组织。
t_en: What a strange arrangement.

t_ch: 其实，我都有点动心……有时我真的很想随心所欲地过一天呀！
t_en: Actually, I'm a little tempted... Sometimes I wish I could spend a day doing whatever I want!

t_ch: 这什么慈善组织！
t_en: Does this count as a charitable organization?

t_ch: 是啊，太贴心了……有时我真的很想随心所欲地过一天呀！
t_en: A good one! So considerate... Sometimes I wish I could spend a day doing whatever I want!

t_ch: 那你就会在自己的舱室里睡一天。
t_en: By which you mean: Spend a day asleep in your cabin.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格的汽车，一种（在你看来）老式的交通工具。
t_en: A Belobog car. An old-fashioned means of transportation (in your eyes.)

t_ch: 这辆汽车的车身冰凉凉的，好像很久没有人驾驶过了。
t_en: The body of the car is cold to the touch. Seems like it hasn't been driven for a long time.

t_ch: 这辆汽车的车窗上积了厚厚的灰，你越往里面瞧，越觉得自己看见了深渊。
t_en: There's a thick layer of dirt on the car window. The more you look at it, the more you feel like you're looking into the abyss.

t_ch: 黑色的车窗向你投以回视，犹如IX从宇宙黑洞的深处投来的一瞥。
t_en: The dark car window gazes back at you, like IX from the depths of a black hole.

t_ch: 车身冒着微微的热气，它的主人似乎刚离开不久。
t_en: Steam can still be seen coming from the car body. Seems like its owner has just left.

t_ch: 这是居民使用的汽车，它们随处停靠，让你非常羡慕这个没有停车费的世界。
t_en: These are the cars used by the residents and they can be parked anywhere. They make you very envious of a world where there are no parking fees.

t_ch: 车牌上画着奇特的符记，你相信这个世界里并没有限号这回事。
t_en: Peculiar marks are drawn on the license plates. You start to think traffic regulations here must be pretty loose.

t_ch: 虽然你已经搭乘过星穹列车这样神奇的交通工具，贝洛伯格的小汽车还是会引起你的注意。
t_en: Despite having traveled on miraculous vehicles like the Astral Express, cars in Belobog still attract your attention.

t_ch: 在某些世界，未成年人是不允许开车的；你相信为了安全，这里也一定如此。
t_en: In some worlds, minors are not allowed to drive. It must be the same here for safety reasons, you think.

t_ch: 铁门冰凉，后面藏着什么事物你不得而知。
t_en: This iron gate is ice cold. You have no idea what's behind it.

t_ch: 这扇铁门仿佛隐藏着全宇宙最重大的秘密。
t_en: This iron gate looks like it's concealing the biggest secret in the universe.

t_ch: 你想坐下，但却失败了。你相信以后一定会成功。
t_en: You want to sit down, but fail. You're sure you'll succeed next time.

t_ch: 空荡荡的长椅上积着雪。
t_en: Snow covers the empty bench.

t_ch: 这盆花生有八瓣，瓣尖有白色斑点，如同即将融化滴落的初雪。
t_en: This flower has eight petals with white dots at the end of each. They look like melting drops of the first snow.

t_ch: 你看见一种细细长长的植物，杆茎呈柱形，内有孔洞。
t_en: You see a tall but thin plant with columnar-shaped stems, the inside full of holes.

t_ch: 一种红黄交间的小花，形状很像太阳。
t_en: A little flower shaped like the sun, featuring both red and yellow.

t_ch: 你看见一只青色的球体，厚厚的瓣叶遮住了它的花蕊，看上去平平无奇。
t_en: You see a cyan sphere. Thick petals and leaves cover its stamen. Nothing special about it.

t_ch: 一种五颜六色的小花，看模样就很便宜。
t_en: A colorful flower. It looks pretty cheap.

t_ch: 你好奇「侯爵夫人」是哪一朵花，但显然它不在这里。
t_en: You wonder which flower is the Marquise, but it doesn't seem to be here.

t_ch: 你看见牌子上写着：「『侯爵夫人』——本店特产，永不打折！」
t_en: You see a sign that says: "The Marquise is our specialty — no discounts!"

t_ch: 你看见牌子上写着：「『初雪八落』——恒温保存，家装佳品！」
t_en: You see a sign that says: "First Snow — easy to maintain, best home decoration!"

t_ch: 你看见牌子上写着：「『覆夏竹』——寓意吉祥，价格亲民！」
t_en: You see a sign that says: "Summershade Bamboo — auspicious and affordable!"

t_ch: 你看见牌子上写着：「『太阳花』——家有一盏，欣欣向荣！」
t_en: You see a sign that says, "Solarflower — you only need one for your place in the sun!"

t_ch: 你看见牌子上写着：「『球牡丹』——年开一月，过时不候！」
t_en: You see a sign that says: "Ball Peony — one month-bloom, don't miss it!"

t_ch: 你看见牌子上写着：「『七彩虹』——七彩缤纷，买一送六！」
t_en: You see a sign that says: "Rainbows — blazing with color, buy 1 get 6 free!"

t_ch: 一位银鬃铁卫把守着向下的道路。
t_en: A Silvermane Guard is keeping watch on the downhill road.

t_ch: 银鬃铁卫冷冷地注视着你，发出了无声的驱赶令。
t_en: The Silvermane Guard stares at you coldly, conveying a silent order to go away.

t_ch: 守卫对你的到来表示出不耐，显然他不欢迎你的靠近。
t_en: The guard seems unhappy at your arrival. Apparently, he didn't welcome your approach.

t_ch: 不知为什么，一个稚嫩的女声突然在你脑海中响起：下面的区域，以后再来探索吧！
t_en: For some reason, a sweet female voice pops into your head: "How about we explore the area ahead of us later?"

t_ch: 银鬃铁卫看守着道路。这人一整天都要站在这里，也太无聊了吧！——而且，这对膝盖可不好啊。
t_en: The Silvermane Guard is keeping watch on the road. They had to stand here all day — must be bored to death! Not to mention, that's really bad for your knees.

t_ch: 银鬃铁卫一动不动，像座雕像。你不禁想：也许这名银鬃铁卫遭到了排挤与打压，才被分配到这里。
t_en: The Silvermane Guard is standing still like a statue. You can't help wondering whether their deployment here isn't the result of an argument with someone further up the chain of command...

t_ch: 你脑补了这位银鬃铁卫毕业，分配，立功，少年得志，得罪小人，发配，流放的全过程。
t_en: You begin to fill in the life of this Silvermane Guard for yourself: Their graduation, deployments, meritorious contributions, youthful brilliance... how they offended some loser, got demoted, thrown out in the cold...

t_ch: 等下，这真的是活人吗？不会是雕像吧。
t_en: Wait, is that definitely a person? Don't tell me it's a statue.

t_ch: 哎呀，好像还真是雕像。
t_en: Oh for the love of... Looks like it really is a statue.

t_ch: 你注意到随着你的接近，守卫的眼神变得炽热起来。
t_en: As you approach, you see a light flicker in the guard's eyes.

t_ch: 太好了，不是雕像。
t_en: Phew, still a person.

t_ch: 一座售卖食物的小屋，屋子里没有人。
t_en: A hut that sells food with no one inside.

t_ch: 这是广场的小卖部。你意外地发现：对面也有一座小卖部。
t_en: It's a small shop in the plaza. Unexpectedly, there's another one on the opposite side.

t_ch: 广场上有两座小房子，一个是小卖部，另一个也是小卖部。
t_en: There are two huts in the plaza. One is a small shop, and the other is... also a small shop.

t_ch: 小卖部里的食物吸引了你，但是这个世界还没有资本家推行无人售货。
t_en: The food in the shop draws you in, but apparently this world has yet to conceive its first self-service promoting capitalist.

t_ch: 你看见菜单上写着：「哞哞特种奶，每日新鲜奶！——2元/瓶。」
t_en: You see on the menu: "Fresh daily Moo Milk! 2 shield per bottle."

t_ch: …连值班的人都没有，谁送这新鲜奶呢？
t_en: ...What use are fresh milk deliveries if there's no one here to sell it?

t_ch: 你看见菜单上写着：「贝洛伯格速食汤罐！营养好喝，各种口味！——葡萄味4元/罐。」
t_en: You see on the menu: "Belobog Instant Soup! Delicious and nutritious in all sorts of flavors! Grape flavor: 4 shield per tin."

t_ch: …其它口味呢？
t_en: ... What about the other flavors?

t_ch: 你看见菜单上写着：「巡游花圃蜂蜜酿！全世界最好喝的蜂蜜饮料！——售罄。」
t_en: You see on the menu: "Parterre Patrol Honey Drink! The world's greatest honey-based beverage! Sold out."

t_ch: …太可惜了，你本来还想尝尝。
t_en: ... What a pity. You wanted to try some.

t_ch: 你看见菜单上写着：「罗杰饮料，水的价格，面包的味道！——5元/瓶」
t_en: You see on the menu: "Roger's Drink — the taste of bread for the price of water! 5 shield per bottle."

t_ch: …这个世界的水还真贵啊。
t_en: ... Water in this world is so expensive.

t_ch: 你看见菜单上写着：「一串香！吃一串想两串，吃两串想九串！一天不吃浑身难受！——店主回工厂区省亲了，请改日再来。」
t_en: You see on the menu: "Tasty Skewers! You can't live without them! Owner visiting family in the factory district. Please come back another day."

t_ch: …那些浑身难受的人真可怜啊。
t_en: ...Too bad for those who can't live without them.

t_ch: 你看见菜单上写着：「火淇淋，传说中的夏天味道。——1元/根。警告：辣椒过敏者禁止购买。」
t_en: You see on the menu: "Fire Chillin' Ice Cream. Legendary taste of the summer. 1 shield each. Warning: Forbidden for those with chili allergies."

t_ch: …火淇淋，听着好像丹恒描述过的一种神兽。
t_en: Fire Chillin' Ice Cream... sounds like a mythical creature Dan Heng once described.

t_ch: 红色的邮筒，筒口露出白信封的一角。这只信封很不幸地卡在邮筒当中。
t_en: A red mailbox with the corner of a white letter sticking out. This letter is unfortunately stuck.

t_ch: 拿出来。
t_en: Take it out.

t_ch: 你小心翼翼地用手指夹住信封，把它拿了出来。
t_en: You carefully nip the letter out with your fingers.

t_ch: 推进去。
t_en: Push it in.

t_ch: 你轻轻一触，信封失去了平衡，消失在邮筒的漆黑深处。你感到自己的善恶值——如果世界上有这种东西的话——提升了。
t_en: You touch the letter lightly. It tips over immediately and disappears into the dark of the mailbox. You feel like your karma (if that even exists in this world) just improved.

t_ch: 不管它。
t_en: Leave it.

t_ch: 你耸耸肩，离它而去。世界上每时每刻都有人出生，患病，死去，宇宙中未被妥投的邮件数以亿计，这一只又特殊在哪里？
t_en: You shrug and leave. Every passing moment, people are being born into this world, getting sick in this world, dying in this world. There are countless letters that don't make it to their recipients. What's so special about this one?

t_ch: 总有人会来处理它——其实，你很确定，当你下次来到这个邮筒时，就不会再看见它了。
t_en: Someone will come and deal with it. In fact, you're certain you won't see that letter again the next time you come to this mailbox.

t_ch: 信封上写着：好心人，能帮我买张邮票贴上，再帮我寄出去吗？千恩万谢！
t_en: The envelope includes the following: Dear kindhearted soul, can you please buy me a stamp and post this for me? A thousand thanks!

t_ch: 你打开信封，里面是一封写在报纸边缘的短信：姐姐，请速寄<unbreak>50</unbreak>元来！——你最爱的迪迪。
t_en: You open the letter. There's a short message on the edge of some newspaper: "Sister, please send me <unbreak>50</unbreak> shield ASAP! Love from your dear brother."

t_ch: 这是一只红色的邮筒，似乎最近刚刚被邮差清理过。它挺立在街边的模样是那么孤独。
t_en: It's a red mailbox. Seems like the mailman just finished with it. It stands on the street all alone.

t_ch: 这只邮筒似乎没有什么特别之处，但你总觉得哪里不对劲。仔细一看，你发现一只白色信封孤零零地躲在它的影子里。
t_en: There's nothing special about the mailbox, but you feel like something's wrong. You look at it carefully again, and see a white letter hidden in its shadow.

t_ch: 投入邮箱。
t_en: Put it in the mailbox.

t_ch: 你拾起信封，将它投入邮箱。在内心深处，你感到某个崇高的存在冲你微微一笑，眨着眼睛竖起拇指——就像在用跳眼法测量爆炸距离。
t_en: You pick up the letter and put it in the mailbox. From deep in your heart, you feel a noble existence smile, wink, and give you a thumbs up — like a soldier trying to gauge an explosion radius.

t_ch: 悄悄收起。
t_en: Take it away quietly.

t_ch: 你收起信封。
t_en: You take the envelope.

t_ch: 放任不管。
t_en: Leave it.

t_ch: 你耸耸肩，离它而去。世界上每时每刻都有人出生，患病，死去，宇宙中未被妥投的邮件数以亿计，这一只又特殊在哪里？
t_en: You shrug and leave. Every passing moment, people are being born into this world, getting sick in this world, dying in this world. There are countless letters that don't make it to their recipients. What's so special about this one?

t_ch: 总有人会来处理它——其实，你很确定，当你下次来到这个邮筒时，就不会再看见它了。
t_en: Someone will come and deal with it. In fact, you're certain you won't see that letter again the next time you come to this mailbox.

t_ch: 信封上写着：行政区，六号街，三十五栋B<unbreak>03</unbreak>室，卡尔•艾尔先生收。
t_en: The envelope reads: To Carl Eyre, Room B<unbreak>03</unbreak>, Building <unbreak>35</unbreak>, 6th Street, Administrative District.

t_ch: 垃圾桶，没有勇气的人不得靠近。
t_en: A trashcan. Don't get close without courage.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格的垃圾桶，藏匿东西的最好地点。
t_en: A Belobog trashcan. Great place to hide something.

t_ch: 垃圾桶。人生失意的时候，你曾经觉得自己和它很相配。
t_en: A trashcan. Once, when you were feeling down on your luck, you thought the two of you were a good match.

t_ch: 这只是个垃圾桶。
t_en: It's just a trashcan.

t_ch: 你什么也没有错过，垃圾桶里没有值得注意的东西。
t_en: You haven't missed anything. Nothing worth looking at in this trashcan.

t_ch: 调查垃圾桶似乎并没什么意义。
t_en: There doesn't seem much point in investigating a trashcan.

t_ch: 平平无奇的垃圾桶。
t_en: A trashcan, not remarkable in any way.

t_ch: 你盯着垃圾桶看了许久，但这还是平平无奇的垃圾桶。
t_en: You've been staring at it for a while, but it's just an ordinary trashcan.

t_ch: 呃…你想干什么？
t_en: Ugh... What do you want to do?

t_ch: 你克制住打开它的冲动。
t_en: You try to hold back the urge to open it.

t_ch: 你盯着垃圾桶看了许久，它们好像变了，寒铁桶边不再锈迹斑斑，桶身上的凹陷也平整了许多。
t_en: As you continue staring at the trashcans, they seem to turn before your very eyes. The edges are no longer rusty and the dents are smoothed over.

t_ch: 从桶盖下微微透出金色的光芒——甜蜜而诱人。
t_en: From under the lid comes a faint golden glow, sweet and alluring.

t_ch: 有一瞬间，你甚至觉得垃圾桶变成了宝箱。
t_en: For a moment, the trashcans turn into treasure chests.

t_ch: …这一刻还是来了。
t_en: ...And it's happening again.

t_ch: 你深吸一口气，伸出手，掀开桶盖：里面什么都没有——
t_en: You take a deep breath and open the lid: It's empty...

t_ch: 不对，你弯腰把手伸进垃圾桶里，从桶底捡起一块铁片。功夫不负有心人，你终于找到了宝藏！
t_en: Wait! You reach deeper into the trashcan. There's a piece of iron scrap on the bottom. Your hard work paid off! You finally found the treasure!

t_ch: 你回过头，只见同伴们神情复杂地看着你。
t_en: You look back at your companions and see their complicated expressions.

t_ch: …不要解释，我明白，对你来说这种冲动太强烈啦。
t_en: ...You don't need to explain. I get it. That urge is too great for you to resist.

t_ch: 人一旦踏上这条路，就回不了头了。
t_en: There's no turning back once you've walked down this path.

t_ch: 这只垃圾桶呈现出某种从容不迫的优雅魅力，散发着不容置喙的超凡自信，这或许与它收藏的一封信件碎片有关。
t_en: There is a leisurely elegance to the trashcans, which exudes commanding confidence. It may have something to do with the letter fragment in its possession.

t_ch: 你绕着邮筒看了一圈，没有再找到别人遗落的信件。
t_en: You walk around the mailbox inspecting it but don't find any more letters.

t_ch: 一只普普通通的邮筒。
t_en: Just a normal mailbox.

t_ch: 几个罐头，标签写着「贝洛伯格红肠」；
t_en: A few tins labeled "Belobog Sausage."

t_ch: 一瓶未开封的吉姆•罗杰面包味汽水；
t_en: An unopened bottle of Jim Roger bread soda.

t_ch: 一张「水晶日报」，印着大守护者可可利亚的头像，一群银鬃铁卫簇拥在她左右；纸团；
t_en: A copy of the Crystal Daily with Supreme Guardian Cocolia on the front page, surrounded by Silvermane Guards. Crumpled paper ball.

t_ch: 一捧凋谢的花，它的恒温罩子已经碎了。
t_en: Some withered flowers whose thermal dome is broken.

t_ch: 想了解一个城市，就去翻它的垃圾桶。
t_en: To understand a city, go through its trashcans.

t_ch: 两个垃圾桶，一边用来回收干垃圾，一边用来回收湿垃圾。
t_en: Two trashcans. One for recycling dry garbage, one for recycling wet garbage.

t_ch: 那么，椰子壳是什么垃圾？
t_en: So... where would you throw a coconut husk?

t_ch: 干垃圾！
t_en: Dry garbage!

t_ch: 没错。椰子壳不会腐烂，没有毒性，也不能进行回收利用，它是干垃圾！
t_en: Correct. Coconut shell doesn't rot, and it's not toxic. You can't recycle it either, so it belongs to the dry garbage!

t_ch: 湿垃圾！
t_en: Wet garbage!

t_ch: 错了！椰子壳虽然是果皮废料，但它质地坚硬，不易粉碎，不能当作湿垃圾处理！
t_en: Nope! Although coconut husk counts as fruit waste, it's hard and doesn't crumble easily. As such, it doesn't go in the wet garbage!

t_ch: 不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 垃圾分类的知识涌入你的脑海。
t_en: Garbage-sorting knowledge comes flooding into your brain.

t_ch: 你沉浸在分类垃圾的快乐里……
t_en: You immerse yourself in the joys of sorting garbage...

t_ch: 这只垃圾桶的内部光洁如新，一尘不染，你从没见过这么干净的垃圾桶——
t_en: The inside of this trashcan is good as new. You've never seen a trashcan this clean before...

t_ch: 不如说，你从没见过比它更干净的东西。这只垃圾桶仿佛全宇宙的纯洁化身，这未免也太荒诞了吧？
t_en: In fact, you've never seen anything this clean before. This trashcan is like the universe's very incarnation of purity... Maybe we're going too far.

t_ch: 抚摩桶身。
t_en: Stroke the trashcan.

t_ch: 你伸出手，手指轻抚桶身。手指传来极度舒适的触感，轻柔、丝滑、无拘无束…
t_en: You stretch your hand out and gently stroke the trashcan. A soft, tender, smooth, unconstrained, and tactile sensation travels up your fingertips...

t_ch: 你感到一种至高无上的满足，就像某个童话故事里睡在九层床垫上的公主。烦恼、疲惫、使命都离你而去……
t_en: You feel a great sense of satisfaction — like that of some fairy tale princess who's just woken up from a night's sleep on nine mattresses stacked on top of each other. All concern, exhaustion, and sense of duty fly away...

t_ch: 毫无疑问，这就是全世界最完美的垃圾桶。
t_en: There's no doubt about it, this is the world's most beautiful trashcan.

t_ch: 仔细端详。
t_en: Investigate carefully.

t_ch: 你定睛观瞧。垃圾桶的内部呈现完美的，毫无杂质的黑暗，纯粹的黑暗——犹如环绕IX的暗物质云团。
t_en: You take a good look at it. The inside of this trashcan is dust-free and perfectly dark. It's the kind of pure darkness that surrounds IX in a nebula of dark matter.

t_ch: 它吸引你的注意，你的眼睛一刻也不能从中离开…你看见了自己，看见三月七和丹恒，星穹列车的命运，未来的终局……
t_en: It focuses your attention, and your eyes become fixed on it... You see yourself. You see March 7th and Dan Heng. You see the fate of the Astral Express and the end of the future...

t_ch: 你强迫自己移开目光，一切都消失了，你觉得自己好像做了一场幻梦。
t_en: You force yourself to stop staring and everything disappears. It feels like a fantasy dream.

t_ch: 关上盖子。
t_en: Close the lid.

t_ch: 全世界最完美的垃圾桶离开你的视线，只有你知道这究竟需要多大的勇气。
t_en: Only you know how much courage it takes to avert your gaze from the world's most beautiful trashcan.

t_ch: 怎么啦，里面是什么？你的表情好怪喔。
t_en: What's up? What's in there? What's with your expression?

t_ch: 「什么也没有。」你决定这么告诉她。
t_en: "Nothing" you decided to tell her.

t_ch: 一个大的出奇的纸团，展开后是几张七皱八褶的纸片。
t_en: An unusually large paper ball. Upon unfolding, you find it contains wrinkled scraps of paper.

t_ch: 一：『这封遗书将是我人生的注脚。因此在生命的最未，我』
t_en: 1. "I hereby tender my resignation. On this, my final day of work, I"

t_ch: 二：『这封遗书将是我人生的注脚。因此在生命的最末，我希望自己足够坦诚。通俗地来说，我觉得无事可千，活着』
t_en: 2. "I hereby tender my resignation. On this, my final day of work, I wish to be frank. Generally speaking, my job is uninteresting, and so"

t_ch: 三：『这封遗书将是我人生的注脚。因此在生命的最末，我希望自己足够坦诚。通俗地来说，我觉得无事可干，活着也没亿忆亦』
t_en: 3. "I hereby tender my resignation. On this, my final day of work, I wish to be frank. Generally speaking, my job is uninteresting, and therefore has no meening"

t_ch: 四：『算了，上班去。』
t_en: 4. "Forget it. Time for work."

t_ch: 错字救人一命。
t_en: A few typos away from a new career...

t_ch: 垃圾桶里躺着一张皱巴巴的手写传单，上面的字迹隐约可辨：
t_en: A wrinkled handwritten flyer is lying in the trashcan. You can just make out the following:

t_ch: 「今日本店爱情花全年最低价，是时候送花给你的伴侣啦！」
t_en: "This will be our best price for valentines flowers for the entire year! Take this chance to send your loved one some flowers!"

t_ch: 在所有低情商的宣发文案里，这也可以算是翘楚了。
t_en: Considering all the other cliched advertising, this is pretty good.

t_ch: 哇，要是有人送我这个，我一定气死。
t_en: Wow, I'd be so mad if someone gave me this.

t_ch: 放心，不会有的。
t_en: Don't worry, no one would.

t_ch: 呵呵，很幽默嘛，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Ahaaaa... You're so hilarious, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你喜欢别人送什么？
t_en: What do you like to give as a present?

t_ch: 花呀，送花我就很喜欢～
t_en: Flowers. I love flowers~

t_ch: …但是让我知道是打折、清仓、或者随便捡的处理品，那人就死定了。
t_en: ...If they put it like that and I catch wind that they are on ordinary discount, on clearance, or picked up off the street, then those people are toast.

t_ch: 仅仅靠近这只垃圾桶，浓烈的恶臭就已扑面而来。
t_en: You smell a strong stench as you approach the trashcan...

t_ch: 你仿佛看见它周围紫黑色怪异而扭曲的气场，明确地表示出「生人勿近」的抗拒态度。
t_en: ...almost as if there were some strange, twisted, dark purple aura surrounding it, protesting in no uncertain terms: "no living entities permitted."

t_ch: 即使这样你也要打开它吗？
t_en: Be that as it may, you gonna open it?

t_ch: 打开它。
t_en: Open it.

t_ch: 在队友诧异的眼神中，你憋住呼吸，如同士兵面对枪林弹雨般朝着垃圾桶冲刺前进。
t_en: While your astonished companions look on, you hold your breath and proceed towards the can like a soldier charging into a hail of bullets.

t_ch: 话又说回来，你为什么执着于翻开城市里每只垃圾桶？这能让你得到什么？
t_en: Wait, why are you so interested in rooting through every trashcan in the city? What do you have to gain?

t_ch: 冲锋！
t_en: Chaaaarge!

t_ch: 你抱着必死的决心打开垃圾桶盖，一眼就看见那个散发着浓烈异味的奇怪东西。
t_en: You decided to open the lid of the can with a determined mind. At your first glance, you saw that strange stinky thing.

t_ch: 一瞬间你仿佛老僧入定，眼观鼻，鼻观心，迅速伸手抓住了它。
t_en: Suddenly, like an old monk sitting in concentrated introspection, you reach out and grab it.

t_ch: 但你的内心毫无喜悦。因为同伴纷纷掩起鼻子离开了你。
t_en: However, you fail to reach enlightenment given that your companions just ran for cover holding their noses.

t_ch: 撤退！
t_en: Retreaaaat!

t_ch: 明智之举。这世上总归存在着比眼前的事物更为珍贵的东西。你虽然不知道那是什么，但此时此刻它是阻止你越过那一线的理由。这就已经足够。
t_en: Wise choice. There must be something better out there in the world than what's in front of you. You don't know what that is yet, but it just stopped you from crossing an important line, which is good enough.

t_ch: 我放弃。
t_en: I give up.

t_ch: 明智之举。你退开十步以外，深深呼吸，让新鲜的空气重新充盈鼻腔。没错，这才是人生，这才是活着，活着就要懂得放弃。
t_en: Wise choice. You back away ten paces, take a deep breath, and let some fresh air into your lungs. Yes, this is real life, this is living. In life, you need to know how to give up.

t_ch: 好看的垃圾桶，但也只是个垃圾桶。
t_en: A nice-looking trashcan, but that's all it really is: A trashcan.

t_ch: 一封黑底烫银的硫酸纸信封。如果它出现在邮筒旁，你会尽情猜度这是一封投错地址的情书，一张实为广告传单的优惠券…但这儿是个垃圾桶。
t_en: A tracing paper envelope, black with silver lettering. If you found it next to a mailbox, you'd speculate that this flyer coupon was a love letter sent to the wrong address... but this is a trashcan.

t_ch: 上面写着：「今天是守护者阿丽萨的生日，收到这封信的人必须在一天内将此信转寄给三个人，否则一年霉运缠身。」
t_en: It reads: "Today is Guardian Alisa's birthday. The recipient of this letter must forward it to three other people within a day, or bad luck will follow them for a whole year."

t_ch: 你好奇把它扔进垃圾桶的人现在怎样了。
t_en: You wonder what happened to the person who threw it away.

t_ch: 有人把一本书扔进了垃圾桶，你捡起来。这似乎是一部长篇连载小说，书名叫《雪国冒险奇谭》，你手中的残本是第四卷。
t_en: Someone threw a book into the trashcan. You pick it up. It seems to be a lengthy serial novel by the name of Tale of the Winterlands. You seem to have the incomplete 4th volume in your hands.

t_ch: 你走近垃圾桶时，微微的焦味窜入你的鼻子。
t_en: A burning smell invades your nostrils as you approach the trashcan.

t_ch: 这只垃圾桶好似被火焚烤过，桶身扭曲变形，黑色仿佛焦灰的物质在桶底铺了淡淡一层，光线照进来时，它们轻轻飘浮，像在跳舞。
t_en: This trashcan looks like it was set on fire. The entire trashcan is deformed, with a layer of dark, scorched, ash-like substance coating the bottom. In the light, you see the particles floating softly, almost dancing.

t_ch: 垃圾桶里躺着一张漂亮的明信片，上面写着：「朋友，你过界了，这不是你应该触碰的东西。署名：垃圾之王。」
t_en: A pretty postcard is lying in the trashcan. It reads: "Friend, you've crossed the line. This isn't something you should have touched. Signed, Garbage King."

t_ch: 明信片背面粘着一枚硬币，看来这位国王挺懂规矩。
t_en: There is a coin glued to the back of the postcard. Seems like this king understands that you need to tip someone if you want them to pass on a message.

t_ch: 你在翻垃圾领域遇到了竞争对手。
t_en: Seems like you've got yourself a real competitor when it comes to rooting through trashcans.

t_ch: 怎么会有人会管自己叫垃圾之王？他自己就不觉得丢人吗？
t_en: Why would he call himself Garbage King? Isn't he embarrassed?

t_ch: 或许你已经发现了：是的，这儿的垃圾桶多到超乎想象。曾经也有人提出减少垃圾桶的投放数量，或是开征垃圾税之类的政策。
t_en: You may have noticed that yes, there are an unbelievable number of trashcans in this city. Somebody did once propose to reduce the overall number, or to initiate some kind of garbage tax policy.

t_ch: 但只是徒增街道上的垃圾。如今这座城市的人们已经达成共识：垃圾桶，是贝洛伯格的另一张名片。
t_en: But it only increased the amount of garbage on the street. Now people in this city have come to the consensus that trashcans are Belobog's other business card.

t_ch: 两个垃圾桶，一边用来回收干垃圾，一边用来回收湿垃圾。
t_en: Two trashcans. One for recycling dry garbage, one for recycling wet garbage.

t_ch: 帕姆掉的毛是什么垃圾？
t_en: Where would you throw Pom-Pom's fur?

t_ch: 干垃圾！
t_en: Dry garbage!

t_ch: 答对了！帕姆的毛发难以腐烂，没有毒性，属于干垃圾！
t_en: Correct! Pom-Pom's fur doesn't easily degrade and isn't toxic, so it belongs in the dry garbage!

t_ch: ——清醒点吧，你的小玩意没那么大破坏力。
t_en: *sigh* ...Come on, those toys of yours aren't that dangerous.

t_ch: 呃，那为啥{F#她}{M#他}还没醒呢？
t_en: Uh, then why hasn't {F#she}{M#he} woken up yet?

t_ch: 因为{F#她}{M#他}睡着啦，刚刚还嘟囔着说梦话呢…真可怜，一定是非常可怕的噩梦。
t_en: Because {F#she's}{M#he's} sound asleep — didn't you hear {F#her}{M#him} sleep talking? ...Poor child, sounded like a bad nightmare.

t_ch: 跟我说实话，桑博，你打算怎么处理那个上层区的小丫头？
t_en: Tell me the truth Sampo — what are you gonna do about the Overworld girl?

t_ch: 怎么处理？和其他几个一起处理呗，找机会给送回去…等等，你为什么——
t_en: What am I gonna do about her? Why, wait for the opportunity and send her back together with the rest of them of course, I... Hey come on, what's with the—

t_ch: 你觉得能瞒得住我吗，桑博•科斯基。
t_en: You're a bad liar, Sampo Koski.

t_ch: …我没想带上她，烟雾太重了，我也迷迷糊糊的，不知怎么就带下来了。
t_en: Wha— ...I didn't mean to bring her along! The smoke was too dense, I was in a daze! Before I knew it, I'd somehow dragged her down here too.

t_ch: 真不知道你想搞什么鬼，桑博。上层的事和你有什么关系，干嘛非要趟这摊浑水？你还嫌自己不够显眼，非得被「地火」盯上才满意？
t_en: What are you up to, Sampo? Why do you insist on getting caught up in Overworld affairs that have nothing to do with you? If it's a whole lot more attention from Wildfire you want, you're going about it the right way.

t_ch: 别这么说嘛！我桑博一向关照朋友，这几人帮了我的忙，怎么也得把人情还上啊。
t_en: Hey now! Sampo Koski's primary concern is taking care of his friends. These guys scratched my back. How could I live with myself if I didn't scratch theirs?

t_ch: 再说了，「地火」又怎么…没准我带来的这几位人物能捞「地火」一把呢，这可不好说嘛。
t_en: And another thing! Where does Wildfire get off... assuming that these fine folks won't come in useful to them, huh? I mean, you never know.

t_ch: 这就是你放任他们几个在地底乱跑的理由？
t_en: Which is why you've set them loose in the underground?

t_ch: 哎哟，意外，纯属意外！我这就去把他们都找回来。
t_en: Come on, accidents happen! I'll have them rounded up in no time.

t_ch: 那个小姑娘…你最好赶紧找到人，把她看住了。地下被封锁十几年，孩子都快忘记地上人长什么样了，突然冒出个穿银鬃铁卫衣服的姑娘……
t_en: *sigh* That girl... get her back as soon as possible, then don't let her out of your sight. The underground has been sealed off for over ten years now — the children won't even remember what overgrounders look like. If a girl in a Silvermane Guard uniform suddenly shows up...

t_ch: 你想想「地火」要是知道了，会怎么对她，怎么对你？
t_en: ...what would Wildfire do with her? What would they do with you?

t_ch: 我懂，我懂！我这就去找人——大姐头，咱这位「客人」就劳烦您受累照顾了！
t_en: Alright, I get it! I'm setting off right now. I'll have to trouble you to look after them while I'm gone, ma'am.

t_ch: 小瞌睡虫，你可算睡醒了啊。
t_en: ...Hey sleepyhead, looks like you're awake.

t_ch: …他们会痛苦，会牺牲，会因我的命令而死。
t_en: ...They will suffer, sacrifice, and die because of my order.

t_ch: 恭迎他们的并非死亡…他们会被裹入新世界的襁褓。
t_en: It is not death which greets them... They will be swept into the infancy of a new world.

t_ch: 那下层的人民呢？他们会失去筑城者的庇护。他们会视我为…暴君。
t_en: What about the Underworlders? They will lose the protection of the Architects. They will see me... as a tyrant.

t_ch: 你见过了…我们承诺的世界。
t_en: You have witnessed... the world we have promised.

t_ch: 短暂的误解…换来永恒的繁荣。
t_en: A temporary misunderstanding... for an eternity of prosperity.

t_ch: 由你决定…我们尊重。
t_en: We will honor... your decision.

t_ch: 我明白了。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 感觉怎么样？身体有哪里不舒服吗？
t_en: How are you feeling? Any aches or pains?

t_ch: 没有，舒服极了。
t_en: No, I feel much better.

t_ch: 那就好。你一直不醒，说些奇怪的梦话，我都开始担心了呢。
t_en: Good. You were out for a while, I was beginning to worry. You were saying some strange things.

t_ch: 我全身头疼。
t_en: I've got migraines all over my body.

t_ch: …希望你是在开玩笑。虽然比喻挺精准的，但正常人可不这么说话。
t_en: ...I hope you're joking, hehe... That's either an interesting metaphor, or you hit your head.

t_ch: 我做了个怪梦…
t_en: I had a weird dream...

t_ch: 是啊，你一直在说什么「星核」，「愿望」…净是些听不明白的话。
t_en: I bet, you were mumbling something about "Stellarons" and "wishes"... I couldn't make sense of it.

t_ch: 既然醒了就活动活动身子吧。我是娜塔莎，地下的医生。你已经在我的诊所里躺了一整天了。
t_en: Well, now that you're awake, you should probably try moving your limbs. I'm Natasha — a doctor in the underground. You've already spent a day in my clinic.

t_ch: 你好，我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Nice to meet you, I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你好…呵呵，你还挺有礼貌的。
t_en: Nice to meet you too... Hehe, you're very polite.

t_ch: 这里是地下？
t_en: Is this the underground?

t_ch: 是啊，就是被他们地上人封住不管十几年的地下。跟上面的花花世界不一样，这里的住民靠挖隧道跟采矿过活。
t_en: Yes... it's a place that's been sealed off and neglected by the overgrounders for more than a decade. The underground has none of the splendor of the surface — the people here dig tunnels and mine ore to make a living.

t_ch: 这是你的诊所？
t_en: This is your clinic?

t_ch: 嗯哼，地下的环境很不好，这里的人很容易生病，而我就是他们唯一的医生。
t_en: Mm-hmm, I'm afraid the conditions underground are terrible. People get sick easily here, and I'm the only doctor.

t_ch: 桑博去收拾自己惹的烂摊子了，我先替他照料你。不过看你这样子，脑袋和身体应该都没什么情况，很健康。
t_en: Sampo's gone to clear up a mess of his own making, so I'm looking after you in the meantime. Based on your current state, I don't think there's any cause for alarm — you seem perfectly healthy.

t_ch: 如果你没什么事的话，我就先去照顾其他病人啦？
t_en: So if you don't mind, I'll go and take a look at the other patients?

t_ch: 我是怎么昏倒的？
t_en: Why did I pass out?

t_ch: 桑博告诉我你们晕倒了，没说细节，多半是他搞的鬼吧，他经常鼓捣些奇奇怪怪的玩意儿。
t_en: Sampo just told me that you lost consciousness. He didn't say why. Still, I'll wager it had something to do with him — he's always tinkering with his devices.

t_ch: 不过从诊断结果来看，你们的身体没有异常。
t_en: Anyway, the opinion of the clinic is that none of you have come to harm.

t_ch: 有人在追捕我们…
t_en: People were following us...

t_ch: 桑博跟我说了。别担心，银鬃铁卫不会追到地下来的。地上和地下已经隔绝很多年了，地下最混乱的时候铁卫也没来过。
t_en: Sampo told me. Don't worry, the Silvermane Guards won't follow you below ground. We've been cut off from the surface for many years. Even when things were at their worst down here, they never showed.

t_ch: 当然，我不知道筑城者下达封城令的原委，也说不定封锁突然解除了呢？那样的话，你们就帮「地火」完成了他们最想做的事啦。
t_en: ...Of course, I don't know the whole story behind why the Architects ordered the lockdown. Who knows, thanks to you, they might finally be about to lift it... something that Wildfire's wanted to achieve for a long time now.

t_ch: 该付多少医药费…
t_en: What's our medical bill...

t_ch: 啊，那个小意思，没事。说实话，你们几个都只是昏了过去，所以其实我没做什么。
t_en: Oh, please, you needn't worry. You've just been asleep for a while, I barely had to lift a finger.

t_ch: 不过我告诉桑博的版本就稍微夸张了点，他可欠我不少呢！这回总该给我弄点药品回来啦。
t_en: I... might've exaggerated your condition for Sampo's ears... but that guy owes me. He better bring me back some medicine.

t_ch: 我的同伴在哪里？
t_en: Where are my friends?

t_ch: 他们醒得比你早。黑头发的青年是第一个，桑博把他带走了。
t_en: They've been up for a while already. The young guy with the dark hair left first — he went with Sampo.

t_ch: 那个有点闹腾的女孩子，醒过来没多久就跑出去了，估计在附近转悠吧？
t_en: Your rowdier friend took off soon after waking up. I imagine she'll be taking a stroll nearby.

t_ch: 还有个铁卫打扮的小姑娘，我特地嘱咐桑博看住她。但她趁桑博出门的时候，一眨眼就不见了。
t_en: As for the girl in the Silvermane Guard uniform, I told Sampo to watch her... but it seems that he left his post and she made a run for it.

t_ch: 桑博到底是什么人？
t_en: Who is this Sampo anyway?

t_ch: 谁知道呢，神神秘秘的。他自称是倒货商，也不晓得在地下有什么货供他倒，但那家伙确实有不少门路。
t_en: Good question... he's a man of mystery. He claims to be a simple cross-border businessman — not that there's much to interest an Overworlder down here. Either way, he's got a surprising number of connections.

t_ch: 他帮我弄到了很多急需的药品，也帮过「地火」不少忙…至少，他应该没什么坏心眼吧。
t_en: I've managed to secure more than a few urgent medical supplies through him, and he's been a big help to Wildfire... In any case, I don't think his intentions are sinister.

t_ch: 我得去跟他们会合…
t_en: I need to meet up with them.

t_ch: 好啊，不要勉强自己哦？要是哪里不舒服，赶紧回来告诉我。
t_en: Sure, but don't push yourself. If you start feeling unwell, come straight back and tell me.

t_ch: 如果遇上困难，可以试试找「地火」帮忙。
t_en: ...And if you run into any trouble, you can try asking for Wildfire.

t_ch: 「地火」？
t_en: Wildfire?

t_ch: 地下的民间组织，你就当成是地底的银鬃铁卫吧——只是比他们更有人情味些。
t_en: It's a grassroots organization. Think of it as the Silvermane Guards of the underground, but less stone-hearted.

t_ch: 谢谢你，娜塔莎医生。
t_en: Thank you, Dr. Natasha.

t_ch: 大恩不言谢，告辞。
t_en: I can't thank you enough. Bye for now.

t_ch: 用不着谢我，治病疗伤是医生的天职。
t_en: No need to thank me. It's a doctor's duty to heal.

t_ch: 地上和地下已经隔绝了这么多年，突然跑下来你们几个「外来者」，着实是件新鲜事。
t_en: Y'know, the underground has been cut off from the surface for years. For you outsiders to descend on us out of nowhere is pretty incredible.

t_ch: 怎么了，身体有哪里不舒服吗？
t_en: Everything alright? You feeling okay?

t_ch: 想问有关地下的事…
t_en: I want to ask about the Underworld...

t_ch: 下层区？你想了解些什么？
t_en: The Underworld? What do you want to know?

t_ch: 人们为什么生活在地下？
t_en: Why do people live down here?

t_ch: 因为我们需要地髓；贝洛伯格建成后，对能源的需求有增无减。寒潮封闭了对外扩张的可能性，人类只能选择向下发展。
t_en: Because we need Geomarrow. After Belobog's construction, the demand for energy kept increasing. The Eternal Freeze prevented us from expanding outwards, so our only choice was to go down.

t_ch: 在筑城者的号召下，人们进入地底，开凿矿脉建立矿区。至少在上下层封闭之前，在地下生活是一种荣誉。
t_en: The people answered the call of the Architects and descended, excavating ore veins and establishing mines. Before we were sealed off, it was an honor to work underground.

t_ch: 它意味着个人为了整个文明能够存续，自愿选择了一项艰辛的工作。
t_en: It was a choice... the pursuit of arduous labor for the survival of our entire civilization.

t_ch: 下层区的生活状况如何？
t_en: How is life in the Underworld?

t_ch: 一直都不是很好；地下地上的生活环境各有恶劣之处，地上要面对寒潮和裂界的侵蚀，地下则要在物资匮乏的闷热环境里进行体力劳作。
t_en: ...It's never been good. Conditions are tough both above and below the surface. The Overworld is faced with the Eternal Freeze and Fragmentum corrosion, and the Underworld has to contend with hard labor, suffocating heat, and serious shortages.

t_ch: 十多年前银鬃铁卫撤出地下，下层区的状况就更糟了。除了围绕地髓进行的物资交换外，我们和地上几乎断绝了往来。
t_en: It's been over a decade since the Silvermane Guards withdrew from the Underworld, and the situation here has only gotten worse. The only contact we have is the exchange of Geomarrow for goods.

t_ch: 而且，裂界的侵蚀也蔓延到了这里……
t_en: And what's more... the Fragmentum corrosion has begun to reach us down here, too...

t_ch: 我想问些别的事。
t_en: I want to ask something else.

t_ch: 想问有关桑博的事…
t_en: I want to ask about Sampo...

t_ch: 话先说在前面，我对桑博也谈不上有多了解。
t_en: Ask away, but I'm afraid I don't know a lot about him myself.

t_ch: 你是怎么认识的桑博？
t_en: How did you get to know him?

t_ch: 一次精心设计的「偶遇」…吧。桑博总能制造戏剧性的相逢，让人对他印象深刻。
t_en: A meticulously choreographed "chance meeting..." Sampo is pretty good at orchestrating unexpected encounters, and he always leaves a lasting impression.

t_ch: 按他的说法，这是生意人的天赋。
t_en: According to him, that's the gift of a businessman.

t_ch: 你知道桑博去哪里了吗？
t_en: Do you know where he went?

t_ch: 呵呵，这可是个热门问题，要找他「算账」的人多着呢，所以他从不把行踪告诉任何人。
t_en: Hehe, I hear that question a lot — seems like he owes everyone something. That's why he never discloses his whereabouts.

t_ch: 有人需要的时候他就会出现；有人「需要」的时候他就会消失——我只知道这么一个规律。
t_en: When people need him, he appears, and when people really need him, he disappears. Sampo's first and second laws of motion.

t_ch: 我想问些别的事。
t_en: I want to ask something else.

t_ch: 想问有关「地火」的事…
t_en: I want to ask about Wildfire...

t_ch: 我是医生耶，可不是情报贩子…算了算了，你问吧。
t_en: ...I'm a doctor, not an informant! Very well, what's the question?

t_ch: 「地火」是什么？
t_en: What is Wildfire?

t_ch: 「地火」就是一个志愿者性质的民兵组织，就像我说过的，你就当成是地底的银鬃铁卫吧。
t_en: Wildfire is a volunteer grassroots organization. Like I said, you can think of it as the Silvermane Guards of the underground.

t_ch: 他们履行着银鬃铁卫曾经的职责，维护下层区的安全和秩序，与裂界侵蚀争夺地下的生存空间。
t_en: They've taken on the duties of the Guards: Maintain safety and order in the Underworld and compete with Fragmentum corrosion for living space.

t_ch: 我该如何找到「地火」？
t_en: How can I find them?

t_ch: 「哪里有困难，哪里需要帮助，哪里就有『地火』」。
t_en: Wherever there is hardship and a cry for help, there is Wildfire.

t_ch: …你总会遇到他们的。
t_en: ...You can always find them.

t_ch: 没什么。
t_en: I'm fine.

t_ch: 小心点儿，要保重身体哦。
t_en: Okay, take care, and make sure you look after yourself.

t_ch: （得找到三月七跟丹恒…从哪里找起呢？）
t_en: (I gotta find March 7th and Dan Heng... but where should I start?)

t_ch: 搏击…原来地下喜欢这么粗放的娱乐活动啊。
t_en: A club just for fighting... who knew undergrounders had such brutal hobbies.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，很有意思哦！
t_en: Mm-hmm, in fact, it's awesome!

t_ch: 你也参加过吗！？这个俱乐部太不讲武德了吧！
t_en: They let YOU take part!? When did this place last have an inspection!

t_ch: 有很多孩子在那里一起玩哦。
t_en: Loads of kids take part.

t_ch: …不行，我的正义感开始报警了。
t_en: ...Despicable. My "criminal activity" senses are tingling.

t_ch: 到啦，就是这里！
t_en: We're here! This is it.

t_ch: 虎克要去玩搏击了，就不带你们进去啦。
t_en: Hook's gonna go fight. See ya later!

t_ch: 这里是什么人的地盘啊，居然允许小孩子们玩搏击……
t_en: Who's in charge here? How can they just let kids attack each other...?

t_ch: 这里是鼹鼠党的地盘！…不过仅限于小孩子。大人的部分归「地火」管！
t_en: The Moles are in charge, that's who! ...But only of the kids. Wildfire is in charge of grown-up business.

t_ch: 鼹鼠党连这里都管吗？
t_en: So The Moles control this whole area?

t_ch: 呣呣呣～地底都是鼹鼠党的地盘！
t_en: Umm, The Moles control everything underground!

t_ch: 你对「地火」了解多少？
t_en: How much do you know about Wildfire?

t_ch: 呣呣呣～就是…大人的鼹鼠党吧？
t_en: Umm, it's... The Moles for grown-ups...

t_ch: 啊，那边缺个对手～你们要玩搏击的话，就来找我吧！
t_en: Ooh! They need one more player there! I—I gotta go! If you ever wanna fight, come find me!

t_ch: 喂，你要去哪呀？
t_en: Hey, where are you going?

t_ch: 回来回来，他们不会藏那么远的啦！
t_en: Come back, come back, they won't be hiding that far away!

t_ch: 这里好多好多人，虎克好矮，什么都看不到……
t_en: There are so many people here — Hook's too short. I can't see anything...

t_ch: 呃啊！你怎么…你…你没有听到虎克的自言自语吧！
t_en: Wha—! When did you... D—Did you hear what Hook was saying?

t_ch: 我没有听到虎克的自言自语。
t_en: Not a single word.

t_ch: 那就好，你什么也没听见，虎克什么也没说。
t_en: Good. Hook didn't say anything, and you didn't hear it.

t_ch: 我没有听到虎克说自己好矮。
t_en: Not a single word about you saying you're too short.

t_ch: 那就好…你这不是全都听见了吗！
t_en: Hehe, good. Wait a second... You did too hear everything Hook was saying!

t_ch: 哼，大人的搏击还是没有鼹鼠党的有意思，虎克根本不在乎。
t_en: Hmph, grown-up fights are less fun than ours. So what if I can't see!

t_ch: 搏击讲究的是拳法、掌法、指法！缺一不可！必须三者都练到指随心动，随心所欲才行！
t_en: You have to use your fists, palms, and even your fingers in combat! If you don't know how, you're done for! You need to train until your body is an extension of the mind... and the mind is an extension of your will.

t_ch: …我说怎么小孩子也能玩「搏击」，这不就是猜拳吗！
t_en: Fists, palms, and fingers... *gasp* Rock, paper, scissors! Phew! No wonder I was confused!

t_ch: 不得了，听说俱乐部来了个新人，把上届拳王打得满地找牙。
t_en: Did you hear!? There's a new fighter at the club! He knocked the last champ's teeth out!

t_ch: 那找的是牙吗？那找的是拳击牙套。
t_en: Really? At least he still has his mouthguard...

t_ch: 你的幽默感去哪儿了？
t_en: ...Dark sense of humor you got there.

t_ch: 听说俱乐部来了一个黑头发的小哥，打遍天下无敌手，到底什么来历？
t_en: I heard a new fighter showed up at the club — young guy, dark hair. They say he's invincible! Where did he come from?

t_ch: 我听人说，这个小哥是银鬃铁卫的高手。
t_en: I heard someone say he's an elite Silvermane Guard.

t_ch: 胡说八道，银鬃铁卫早就集体撤走了，怎么可能突然回来一个。
t_en: Don't be ridiculous. The Silvermane Guards have been gone for ages. Why would one suddenly appear out of the blue?

t_ch: ——兄弟姐妹们！接下来这场，将是今天最精彩，最刺激，最震撼的对决！
t_en: ...Brothers and sisters! Are you ready for today's most spectacular, spine-tingling, earth-shattering contest!?

t_ch: 不苟言笑，实力超群的超级新人——「冷面小青龙」！由深蓝帅哥推荐！
t_en: On one side — recommended by Tall, Blue, and Handsome — the unsmiling pulverizing power of the new kid on the block: Cold Dragon Young!

t_ch: 他的对手是——没得感情，易燃易爆的机器小分队！赞美史瓦罗大佬！
t_en: And his opponent is... none other than the unfeeling, incendiary, explosive might of Team Robomatic! All praise to Boss Svarog!

t_ch: 由于没有其他选手愿意与机器小分队对抗，这场比赛只好由「冷面小青龙」独自出战，他本人也放出豪言壮语：「随便」！
t_en: Given that no other fighter was willing to take on the strength of Team Robomatic, Cold Dragon Young will face off against these opponents alone! On learning his fate, the brave young fighter had one thing to say: "Whatever!"

t_ch: 那么，第一千七百五十八届搏击擂主挑战赛半决赛，现在开始！
t_en: And so, let the semifinals of the <unbreak>1758</unbreak>th Fighting King Challenge... begin!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，快！我们去帮他！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, quick! Let's help him!

t_ch: 哦？这是…意外的情况出现了！两位观众闯入了八角笼！{F#她们}{M#他们}似乎想和「冷面小青龙」组队？
t_en: Oh-ho? Brothers and sisters, an unexpected turn of events... Two members of the audience have charged into the octagon! It looks like they want to team up with Cold Dragon Young!

t_ch: …总算醒了？
t_en: ...So you're awake?

t_ch: 是啊，醒来第一件事就是到处找你，这不，看见你有难，{NICKNAME}和我赶来救场啦，感谢我们吧～
t_en: Yeah! The first thing {NICKNAME} and I did was go look for you! And now that we've found you, we're trying to make sure you don't get your butt kicked! You're welcome~

t_ch: 我感受到了他们的热情！这项运动就是这么热血，这么有吸引力！不过我必须重申，搏击比赛是专业的，危险的——
t_en: Brothers and sisters, you can feel their passion! The magnetic pull of this electric sport is undeniable! But let me reiterate: These competitions are for professionals only! Whatever you do kids, don't try this at home!

t_ch: ——但是小青龙选手示意比赛继续！他已经迫不及待了！好啊！看看是这帮家伙的拳头硬，还是机器人的铁皮更硬！
t_en: And Cold Dragon Young is signaling that the contest will go ahead! He's just itching to get into it! And so are we! It's the iron fists of Cold Dragon Young — and friends — versus the iron skin of Team Robomatic!

t_ch: 就算你们不来，我一个人也能解决。
t_en: I can take them on my own, you know.

t_ch: …哎，真是不能指望你的情商。
t_en: ...*sigh* We need to work on your emotional intelligence.

t_ch: 注意防御！
t_en: Hold the line!

t_ch: 统统砸烂！
t_en: Destroy them all!

t_ch: 我想回家！
t_en: I just want to go home!

t_ch: 漂亮的一击！胜利者是「冷面小青龙」——以及两位临时参战的观众！
t_en: Oh! What a beautiful performance! Cold Dragon Young and his last-minute admirers emerge victoriouuuus!

t_ch: 「观众」…就不能给我俩也想个绰号嘛。
t_en: Admirers... How come we don't get cool nicknames!

t_ch: 冷面小粉龙！
t_en: Cold Dragon Younger and Cold Dragon Youngest?

t_ch: 冷面小青龙这种绰号…
t_en: Cooler than Cold Dragon Young...?

t_ch: 呃，那算了，品味太差。
t_en: *sigh* Forget it. I think our taste in nicknames is different.

t_ch: 啊，快看，桑博在那儿！
t_en: Hey! Look! It's Sampo!

t_ch: 得找他讨个说法。
t_en: We've got an explanation to demand.

t_ch: 得想法揍他一顿。
t_en: We've got another butt to kick.

t_ch: 得好好向他道谢。
t_en: We should thank him.

t_ch: 我们追。
t_en: Let's get after him.

t_ch: 所以，这个「地火」平时都干什么呢？
t_en: So... what does Wildfire do all day?

t_ch: 什么都干。维持秩序，主持正义，争取资源，分配物资……
t_en: Everything. They keep the peace, uphold justice, fight for resources, distribute goods...

t_ch: 说到物资，我那点微不足道的面子就是从这儿来的……
t_en: The sliver of trust they have for me has to do with the last one...

t_ch: 咦，约书亚居然没在这儿……
t_en: Huh? Where's Joshua?

t_ch: 这种发亮的矿石是？
t_en: What're those shiny rocks?

t_ch: 噢，这是「地髓」，一种能供暖的矿石。地上地下不是全面封锁了嘛？现在就只有地髓的运输线还在运行了，地底往上送矿石，地上往下送物资。
t_en: Ah, this is Geomarrow, an exothermic ore. The underground and the surface are sealed off from one another, right? Well only the Geomarrow transport line is still running. The underground sends ore to the surface, and the surface sends goods to the underground. At least in theory.

t_ch: 这下层区一大半都是矿民，每天就靠挖这种矿石过活。要是没有「地髓」可烧，这个世界的人早就被冻死了。
t_en: Most undergrounders are miners, and this is what they mine for a living. Y'know, if we didn't have Geomarrow to burn, this world would have frozen to death long ago.

t_ch: 呃，咱们接着走吧，要找的人不在……
t_en: Hmm, let's keep moving. The person I was looking for isn't here...

t_ch: 唔…奥列格那老头也不在，人都哪去了？
t_en: Huh...? Old Oleg isn't here either. Where is everyone? Hehe, hm...

t_ch: 你不是又带我们瞎绕圈吧？
t_en: Gah, so you're leading us on another wild goose chase?

t_ch: 绝对不敢，怎么可能！放一万个心吧，马上就能找到！
t_en: I wouldn't dare! Trust me, we're getting close!

t_ch: 哼…远处那个大建筑是啥……
t_en: *sigh* Huh? What's that huge structure in the distance...

t_ch: 那是「炉心」，也是连结上下层区的支撑柱。以前人们会通过那根柱子上下往来，不过后来……
t_en: Oh, that's the Furnace Core, and it's also the pillar that connects the Underworld and the Overworld. People used to travel up and down that thing, but then...

t_ch: 后来怎么了？
t_en: Then what?

t_ch: 说来话长咯。反正，现在上下层之间的联系几乎断绝了。
t_en: It's a long story. In any case, practically nothing moves up or down anymore.

t_ch: 除了你以外。
t_en: Except you.

t_ch: 唉哟我的好哥哥，说好别提的呢？！算我求求您，千万不要跟别人乱说！
t_en: Oh! C'mon, my dear fellow, I thought we weren't going to discuss this? I beg you, the fewer rumors there are about me, the better!

t_ch: 这个时间段，希儿一般都在这片区域巡逻啊……
t_en: Seele's usually always out on patrol here at this time...

t_ch: 「地火」这群人都干嘛呢？不会是在躲着我吧？
t_en: What's Wildfire up to...? Hmm... are they avoiding me?

t_ch: 喂，你们快看，那是谁！
t_en: Hey, look! Who is that?

t_ch: 那是…布洛妮娅？
t_en: Is that... Bronya?

t_ch: 她也被带下来了？
t_en: She's here too?

t_ch: 看这样子，像是遇到什么麻烦了呀…
t_en: It looks like she's in trouble...

t_ch: 几位姐们儿，哥们儿，别袖手旁观啊！这事可万万不能闹大呀……
t_en: M—My friends, don't just stand there! This drama really doesn't need any more bystanders!

t_ch: 你这么一说，我还挺想看你遭殃的诶。
t_en: Oh? Y'know, it might be interesting to see you get in trouble.

t_ch: 我们最好不要引来过多关注，但也不能放着那孩子不管。
t_en: We should avoid attracting too much attention... but we can't just leave her.

t_ch: 桑博说得对，不能袖手旁观。
t_en: Sampo's right, we can't just stand here.

t_ch: 对，对！就是这个理儿。
t_en: Yeah! Now you're getting it.

t_ch: 丹恒说得对，不能放着不管。
t_en: Dan Heng's right, we can't ignore this.

t_ch: 是！是！丹恒…兄弟深明大义。
t_en: Exactly! My boy Cold Dragon Young — uh... young master Dan Heng speaks with reason.

t_ch: 阿七说得对，回去休息吧。
t_en: March is right. Does anyone have popcorn?

t_ch: 呃，咱就说句玩笑话，你别什么都听我的啊。
t_en: Eh, I was kidding, and get your own opinion!

t_ch: 其实我赞同丹恒的意见，咱们总不能看着可爱的女孩子受欺负吧。
t_en: I agree with Dan Heng, we can't just leave her to the bullies.

t_ch: 说得好！姑娘深明大义啊！
t_en: Well said! The young lady is wise and righteous!
